title: Kings And Rulers
content:
Here lies our sovereign lord, the king,
        Whose word no man relies on,
     Who never said a foolish thing,
        And never did a wise one.

    Said by a courtier of Charles, II. To which the King replied, "That is very true, for my words are my own. My actions are my minister's."
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Kings And Rulers"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
